titlestmt.titleproper: Guide to the Besmilr Brigham Papers
titlestmt.author: by Brooke McManus

publicationstmt.publisher: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

controlnote.p: This encoded finding aid is compliant with the Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines, Version 1.0.3830463

maintenanceagency.agencyname: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: Finding aid description and metadata in Archives at Yale are licensed under a CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. This means that anyone may copy, modify, and distribute this descriptive metadata without restriction or permission. The CC0 license is explained in full on the Creative Commons website: CC0 1.0 Universal (CC0 1.0) Public Domain Dedication. More information is available on our Help page.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Tuesday March 18, 2025 at 00:23

corpname.part: Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library

descriptivenote.p: In English, with some materials in Spanish.

did.abstract: The Besmilr Brigham papers consists of correspondence, writings, notes and drawings, printed material, personal papers, and audiovisual media documenting the life and work of poet Besmilr Brigham. Materials date from 1931 to 2011, with the bulk dating between 1960 and 1990.

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: This collection is open for research.
Box 76 (audiovisual materials): Restricted fragile material. Reference copies may be requested. Consult Access Services for further information.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: Purchased from Heloise Wilson on the Danford N. Barney, Jr. Fund, 2015.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six series: I. Correspondence, 1942-2011, undated. II. Writings, 1948-circa 1990s, undated. III. Notes and Drawings, 1941-circa 1990s, undated. IV. Printed Materials, 1934-circa 1990s, undated. V. Personal and Financial Papers, 1931-2000, undated. VI. Audiovisual Media, 1971-1984, undated.

bioghist.head: Besmilr Brigham (1913-2000)
bioghist.p: Besmilr Brigham was an American poet and author. She was born Bess Miller Moore in Pace, Mississippi on September 28, 1913 and raised in the Rio Grande Valley region in Texas, later adopting the phonetic spelling of her name: "Besmilr." Her maternal grandfather was a member of the Choctaw Nation. She graduated from the University of Mary Hardin-Baylor in Belton, Texas, and studied writing at The New School in New York City.

While Brigham was a prolific writer throughout her career, she was most active in the late 1960s and 1970s, publishing her work under the all-lowercase name "besmilr brigham." During this period, she published two books of poetry, Agony Dance: death of the (Dancing dolls (1969) and Heaved from the Earth (1972). Her work was collected posthumously in Run through Rock: Selected Short Poems of Besmilr Brigham (2000), edited by poet C. D. Wright. She first began publishing her work in 1966 with the submission of the long poem Yaqui Deer to Mexican bilingual quarterly El Corno Emplumado . Her later poems were published in anthologies such as 31 New American Poets (1969) and Rising Tides: 20th Century American Women Poets (1973), and periodicals including The Atlantic , Harper's Bazaar , and The New York Times , as well as Thomas Merton's literary magazine Monks Pond . Her work was recognized with a National Endowment for the Arts Discovery Award grant in 1970. Brigham also wrote short stories; "The Lottery Drawing" was featured in The Southern Review (1982) and Mississippi Writers: Reflections of Childhood and Youth (1985).

Brigham married newspaperman Roy Brigham, with whom she had one daughter, Heloise. From 1950 to the mid-1970s, the family traveled frequently throughout Mexico and Central America, Canada, and Alaska. She lived much of her later life in Arkansas. She died in Las Cruces, New Mexico on September 30, 2000.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: Besmilr Brigham Papers. Yale Collection of American Literature, Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library.

processinfo.head: Processing Information
processinfo.p: Collections are processed to a variety of levels, depending on the work necessary to make them usable, their perceived research value, the availability of staff, competing priorities, and whether or not further accruals are expected. The library attempts to provide a basic level of preservation and access for all collections as they are acquired, and does more extensive processing of higher priority collections as time and resources permit.
This collection received a basic level of processing, including rehousing and minimal organization.

Information included in the Description of Papers note and Collection Contents section is drawn from information supplied with the collection and from an initial survey of the contents. Folder titles appearing in the contents list below are often based on those provided by the creator or previous custodian. Titles have not been verified against the contents of the folders in all cases. Otherwise, folder titles are supplied by staff during initial processing.
This finding aid may be updated periodically to account for new acquisitions to the collection and/or revisions in arrangement and description.

scopecontent.head: Content Description
scopecontent.p: The Besmilr Brigham papers consists of correspondence, writings, notes and drawings, printed material, personal papers, and audiovisual media documenting the life and work of poet Besmilr Brigham. Materials date from 1931 to 2011, with the bulk dating between 1960 and 1990. The collection contains correspondence with Robert Bly, Robert Duncan, Charles Glicksberg, Richard Howard, James Laughlin, Denise Levertov, and Margaret Randall, among other literary figures, and family correspondence. Writings consist mainly of annotated typescript drafts of poetry, short fiction, and novels. Notes and drawings include research on Indigenous cultures, class notes, travel journal entries, and early poetry and prose by Brigham. Printed materials consist of clippings and scrapbooks used as source material for Brigham's stories, as well as catalogs, pamphlets, flyers, and maps. Personal papers include biographies, fellowship and grant applications, records of publication and reviews of Brigham's work, teaching materials, and files on events and programs involving Brigham, such as C. D. Wright's Lost Roads Project . Audiovisual media consists of cassette and open reel tapes of readings, interviews, performance art, and recordings related to an unproduced lyric drama by Brigham, "Games for an Easter Child," and religious ceremonies of Pascua Yaqui Indians.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Besmilr Brigham Papers is the physical property of the Beinecke Rare Book and Manuscript Library, Yale University. Literary rights, including copyright, belong to the authors or their legal heirs and assigns. For further information, consult the appropriate curator.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains Brigham's professional correspondence with editors, publishers, and other literary figures, including Robert Bly, Robert Duncan, Charles Glicksberg, Richard Howard, James Laughlin, Denise Levertov, and Margaret Randall, concerning submissions and publication of her work, poetry readings, grant applications, and teaching projects, although the bulk of the series consists of family correspondence. There is some correspondence with Judith Jones of Knopf, editor of Brigham's poetry collection Heaved from the Earth, and with Dorothy Abbott, editor of the anthology Mississippi Writers: Reflections of Childhood and Youth , which featured poetry and fiction by Brigham. The series additionally contains a large amount of correspondence between Brigham and Forrest Coggan, director of Teatro Internacional and founder of the American Mythic Theatre program, regarding a planned production of her dance lyric drama "Games for an Easter Child."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three subseries: Incoming Letters, Outgoing Letters, and Family Correspondence

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Correspondence can also be found in Series II. Writings, and Series V. Professional and Personal Papers.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains letters to Brigham from editors and publishers, as well as other writers, friends, and associates from California, Mexico, and Central America.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with mixed and unidentified correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: University of Arkansas, with copies of 1948 John Gould Fletcher letters to Brigham
"Arts Council etc. Old names and addresses Readings schedule"
Bellarmine College, regarding Thomas Merton letters
Bryan Public Schools, Texas Commission on the Arts and Humanities
Dallman, Elaine, regarding Woman Poet--The South
Hartley, Margaret L. ( Southwest Review ; includes copy of letter from Joyce Carol Oates praising Brigham's work)

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also "Autobiographical essays & letters," Box 13, folder 189.

did.unittitle: King, Coretta Scott (with Martin Luther King, Jr. memorial program)
Southern Federation of State Arts Agencies
United States. Department of State, and Embassies
"Letters from Jon Webb & Gypsy Lou" [Webb, Jon and Louise; Loujon Press]
Webb, Jon, regarding Insomnia, or the Devil at Large , by Henry Miller
Wier, Dara (University of Alabama), regarding Woman Poet--The South
Yarbrough, Marilyn, and Leonard Randolph (National Endowment for the Arts)
Letters to Brigham regarding poetry readings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts and copies of letters from Brigham to editors, publishers, and individuals at universities and cultural institutions regarding submissions, poetry readings, and various literary or teaching projects. There is also a file of autobiographical essays and letters, dated 1972-1991, referencing her childhood, travels in Europe, the United States, Mexico, and Central America, inspirations for her writing, and background of her Choctaw grandfather, Elisha Emmons, which she researched in Choctaw Nation records during a trip to Oklahoma.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: "My letters written relative to Poetry Readings, Poets/Schools, letters to set dates"

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes copy of "My Statement: My Defense: Women and the Art of Poetry" by Brigham.

did.unittitle: Letters to various correspondents, primarily excerpts
"Letters by besmilr (not sent) and miscellaneous material"
Letters to Forrest Coggan regarding "Easter Child" performance text
Letter to Joe [Murphey?] about Death and the Negrito
Letters to Jack and Marty Marken

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains extensive correspondence between Besmilr and Roy Brigham and their daughter and son-in-law Heloise and Keith Wilson regarding personal and financial matters, Besmilr's writings, submission and publication of her work, and her relationships with editors and other literary figures. They also discuss the Brighams' life and travels in California, Mexico and Central America, the American Southwest, and Arkansas, and attendance at various ceremonies and celebrations of the Yaqui, Apache, and other Indigenous peoples. Some letters between Besmilr and Keith, also a poet, include her thoughts on the technical aspects of writing and advice about his career. The series additionally contains correspondence with Brigham's parents, Bessie and Monroe Moore, grandchildren, in-laws and other family members, and a small amount of unrelated correspondence and papers of the Moore and Wilson families

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into three groupings: Letters from Besmilr and Roy Brigham to Heloise and Keith Wilson, Letters from Heloise and Keith Wilson to Besmilr and Roy Brigham, and Other family correspondence and papers.

did.unittitle: Letters from Besmilr and Roy Brigham to Heloise and Keith Wilson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Includes letters written by the Brighams while they lived in San Francisco and Berkeley, California, Arkansas, Arizona, and Texas, and during their trips to Mexico and Central America from the 1950s through the 1970s. Many letters contain updates on Besmilr's writings and revisions she made, submissions and rejections of her work, and efforts to generate income through poetry readings or teaching positions, as well as Roy's jobs as a typesetter. Other topics include books, films, readings at the San Francisco Poetry Center and The Cellar, the Brighams' travel experiences, and contemporary social and political issues, such as student unrest, racism, and the Vietnam War. Letters sometimes were enclosed with copies of Brigham's poems, essays, and other writings.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters describe readings by authors including Allen Ginsberg, Léonie Adams, Theodore Roethke, Robert Duncan, Robert Creeley, William Stafford, and Bob Kaufman, as well as Brigham's interactions with Ginsberg and Duncan.

did.unittitle: Letters from Heloise and Keith Wilson to Besmilr and Roy Brigham

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Letters from the Wilsons often reference their children and cats, Keith's poetry and courses he taught at New Mexico State University, Heloise's work as an ESL teacher, houseguests, local poetry and fiction readings, mutual literary acquaintances such as Robert Duncan, current events, and travel.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains letters to Brigham from her grandchildren, in-laws, and other relatives and close friends, as well as correspondence of Roy Brigham, and correspondence and papers of Moore and Wilson family members. Included are letters to Keith and Heloise Wilson from writers and friends, among them Howard McCord and Bobby Byrd.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by correspondent, with unsorted correspondence filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: Brigham, Roy, incoming correspondence and "letters returned"
Brigham, Roy, copies of letters regarding Besmilr's Alzheimer's diagnosis
Wilson, Heloise, early letters and photographs
Letters from other family and friends
Third party correspondence with Keith and Heloise Wilson

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists primarily of drafts of poems, short stories, novels, and novellas by Brigham (majority unpublished), and a small amount of printed materials. Most of the drafts are typescripts with corrections in Brigham's hand. The series contains extensive records documenting submission and publication of Heaved from the Earth by Knopf, including multiple poem drafts, page proofs, editorial correspondence, contracts, royalty statements, and reviews. There is also a significant amount of material for the dance lyric drama "Games for an Easter Child," including drafts, correspondence, and notes and drawings based on Brigham's research of Pascua Yaqui traditions and observation of ceremonies in Arizona and Mexico. Other works by Brigham represented in the series are her first published poem, Yaqui Deer , and poetry anthology Agony Dance: death of the (Dancing dolls , as well as poems and stories published in journals such as New Directions , The North American Review , The Southern Review , and Southwest Review . Like Agony Dance , titles of Brigham's works often incorporate open-ended parentheses and other non-traditional punctuation. In addition, the series contains novel drafts by Brigham's husband Roy, and writings of others.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into six subseries: Poetry, Shorter Works, Novels and Novellas, Writings by Roy Brigham, Printed Materials, and Writings of Others.

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: Writings can also be found in Series I. Correspondence, and Series III. Notes and Drawings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains research materials, notes, drafts, page proofs, offprints, inscribed works, and some correspondence.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with miscellaneous and untitled poem collections filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of short stories, essays, and Brigham's writings about her approach to poetry and fiction, as well as an entry for the Encyclopedia of the American Woman titled "Writing and Womanhood."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title, with miscellaneous and untitled writings filed at the end of the sequence.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains drafts of unpublished novels by Brigham, such as Helen: their Virtues Else , Death and the Negrito: The Agony of Jackson Emmons , and KATH: the wicked saint , and novellas including Rudi Anaya and the Jackal .

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by title.

Snouter: a tale of a war dog

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries contains published works by Brigham, some of which are annotated or inscribed.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged chronologically.

"the hurricane," other poems, in The

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This subseries consists of poetry, fiction, and non-fiction by other writers, including Keith Wilson, and a Polish translation of Brigham's poem "Tomb."

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by author.

did.unittitle: Flores, Angel, English translation of "Summits of Macchu Picchu" by Pablo Neruda
France, Anatole, "The Juggler of our Lady"
Gander, Forrest, "The Inflourescence of Variety: Four Iconoclastic Southern Poets," with letter
Lawrence, D. H., and others, poems typed and copied by Brigham
Unknown, "Here is a brief history of Nicaraguan involvement"
Unknown, Polish translation of "Tomb" by Brigham, with letter from Victor Contoski

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of research materials, notes taken in courses with John Gould Fletcher and others at The New School, travel journals, and early drafts of poetry and prose by Brigham. Many loose notebook pages were likely kept in small binders by Brigham at one time. The notes also contain translations, biographical information, and addresses of friends, editors, and publishers. There are additionally some personal documents and notes unrelated to her work.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Assorted notes on family, writing, publishing
"besmilr's ruminations!" and notes on various subjects
Class notes and reading notes on poetry and prose
Class notes and reading notes on poetry and prose, other topics
Drawings, Monte Alban, Aztec, and unidentified
"Fletcher Study File" [What is Poetry: A Study]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages from multiple notebooks with writings and drawings by Brigham on various subjects, possibly originating during a 1959 trip to San Blas, Nayarit, in Mexico. Includes references to Mescalero Apache dancers and the history of Indian reservations in the United States from 1873, as well as drafts of poems, stories, and "The Mirror (a play)," and travel journal entries from Mexico and Central America.

did.unittitle: Loose notes, notebook pages, and ephemera
Loose notes, notebook pages, and ephemera

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Pages removed from notebooks and formerly housed in silverware boxes by Brigham. "Notes used" refers to writings that were later copied or incorporated into typescript drafts. "Notes not used" contain writings that Brigham apparently did not develop further.

did.unittitle: "3 pieces for Janus Ring, Soil Must Mellow, Gaunt Wolves, Death & the Negrito, Mariposo, Marriage is like to Art"
"The Fair, Decoy of the Wild Duck, Playa Encantada, Lopez and the Bull"
"Isidoro, Pangloss Garden, Cassandro, Harry Smith, In my Father's Lying Fallow Field"
Reading notes and drawings, Maya, Yanomami
"References used Kath etc." [class notes, letters from Heloise Wilson to "Mamuver"]

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists mainly of newspaper and magazine clippings Brigham used as source material for her writings. Topics include murder and crime, bullfighting, boxing, Indigenous cultures, politics in Nicaragua, and birds and nature, with three scrapbooks of news clippings on homicides and culture in Mexico. The series additionally contains articles about Brigham, publicity for poetry readings, catalogs, pamphlets, atlases, and museum and travel brochures from Oklahoma.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title, with oversize material and scrapbooks filed at the end of the sequence.

did.unittitle: "Bays of Pigs, also incident in Africa" (from Life magazine)
Blank postcards, tobacco wrappers, and other ephemera
El Palacio , with letter from Heloise Wilson

title.part: Hony for Bears, Words for Vikings

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Two copies of a book of poetry produced by students at Bryan High School, where Brigham was an instructor for the Poetry-in-the-Schools Project in Bryan, Texas.

did.unittitle: "Interesting material from Mexico - Information related to short stories and novels" [also includes invoices and religious memorabilia]
"Interesting material from Mexico - Information related to short stories and novels" [also includes invoices and religious memorabilia]
"Juan Darien" and "La Mort du Grand Favre," annotated by Brigham
Magazine clippings and book fliers - Indigenous cultures, Oklahoma
Magazine and newspaper clippings - art, culture, people
Materials on art and archaeology in Mexico
"My Childhood Home" by Bessye [Bessie] E. Moore
Newspaper and magazine clippings - bullfighting, San Francisco
Robley Wilson book jacket, Brigham notes

relatedmaterial.head: Associated Materials
relatedmaterial.p: See also Coggan, Forrest, Box 2, folders 28-29.

did.unittitle: Travel and museum brochures, hotel stationery from Oklahoma trip
Versification in English Poetry , annotated by Brigham
"The Yaqui Today - A People in Transition," in Arizona Highways , with note from Heloise Wilson
"Year of the Native American" calendar, and "An Indian Prayer," printed by Red Cloud Indian School, Pine Ridge, South Dakota.
Ceremonia del Fuego Nuevo [illustrations of Aztec ceremony]
World War II atlases, U.S. Army publications
Scrapbook - bullfighting, culture, people in Mexico

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series contains correspondence and printed materials related to C. D. Wright's Lost Roads Project , a traveling exhibit (1994-1996) featuring Arkansas writers; biographical materials; bibliographies and records of submissions and reviews of Brigham's work; royalty statements and permissions; fellowship and grant applications; and files pertaining to Brigham's participation in Mississippi Writers Day, the PBS program "United States of Poetry," and Library of Congress poetry recordings. There are also teaching materials and other records for the Poetry-in-the-Schools Project in Bryan, Texas, where Brigham instructed high school students. The series additionally includes government and travel documents, notes, ephemera, photographs, and medical and income tax records.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Arranged alphabetically by folder title.

did.unittitle: Addresses related to publications and readings
Bibliographies and lists of published works from 1966 to 2000
"Directory -- On the Road: for send-outs, etcetteras; Coda"
"Excerpts and some personal data" [includes copies of letters from Brigham]
"Quotes of estimate" and reviews from 1966 to 1974
Send-out lists, records of publication and professional correspondence
Brigham artist file, Texas Commission on the Arts and Humanities
Employment and payroll records of Roy Brigham
Financial and legal records, publishing and teaching
High school and college records and ephemera
"John Richspin's Song," with magazine clippings and unidentified watercolor
"Lists of Grants and awards available to American writers"
Mississippi Writers: Reflection of Childhood and Youth , correspondence, permissions, printed material
Mississippi Writers Day program, Brigham's notes, and author autographs
Mississippi Writers Day mailings and correspondence
Obituaries for Roy and Besmilr Brigham
Photographs from Green Bay Press-Gazette article about Brigham
Photographs of family, friends, and colleagues
"Poems to be used with Mexican-American students at the University of Iowa"
Teaching, research, and other professional papers
Trip to France and The Netherlands

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series consists of open reel and audiocassette tapes, including recordings made by Forrest Coggan related to "Games for an Easter Child" and other performances of Indigenous music; readings from Heaved from the Earth and a radio interview with Brigham at the University of Wisconsin-Green Bay during a 1972 poetry reading tour; and performances by poet and artist Nancy du Plessis.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: Organized into two subseries: Open Reel Tapes, and Cassette Tapes.
Arranged alphabetically by title.
